symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical bilateral proximal muscle aching and weakness (thighs, buttocks, shoulders)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical proximal myopathy is the hallmark distribution of statin muscle toxicity.
PMR also produces symmetrical proximal girdle pain/stiffness in older adults.
Inflammatory myopathies cause symmetrical proximal weakness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset a few weeks after the statin dose was doubled

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Temporal onset after a statin dose increase is a key pointer to a statin cause and increases the dose-dependent myotoxicity risk.
A tight temporal link to a medication change makes a primary inflammatory cause less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Concurrent macrolide antibiotic (potent CYP3A4 inhibitor) taken with the statin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A macrolide inhibits CYP3A4 metabolism of the statin, sharply raising statin exposure and myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk — the interaction driving this presentation.
The interaction escalates the risk that severe myopathy progresses to rhabdomyolysis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urine a normal clear-yellow colour (no dark/tea-coloured urine)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of gross myoglobinuria makes established massive rhabdomyolysis less likely at this moment, but does NOT exclude a rising CK or early muscle injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sensory loss, no numbness/pins-and-needles, no bladder/bowel dysfunction

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute spinal cord compression / cauda equina
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A compressive cord/cauda equina lesion would be expected to produce sensory change and/or sphincter disturbance; their absence argues strongly against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced proximal muscle power (~4/5) with deep muscle tenderness and preserved reflexes on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective proximal weakness and muscle tenderness with intact reflexes fit a myopathic (not neuropathic) process — a clinician-measured finding, sealed and not obtainable over chat.

loinc_ref.display: Creatine kinase, total [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

investigation_details.expected_result: elevated (degree determines myopathy vs rhabdomyolysis severity)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of muscle injury and rhabdomyolysis risk cannot be quantified; evolving rhabdomyolysis may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CK quantifies muscle injury and distinguishes mild myopathy from rhabdomyolysis; pending bloods, unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Renal function (U&E / eGFR) to assess acute kidney injury

investigation_details.expected_result: screen for acute kidney injury from myoglobin
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Early acute kidney injury from rhabdomyolysis could go undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Renal function establishes whether muscle breakdown has caused acute kidney injury; sealed investigation, unavailable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies muscle injury and rhabdomyolysis risk; the single most important blood test here.
Screens for acute kidney injury secondary to muscle breakdown.
Statin toxicity can affect the liver; baseline before/after cessation is appropriate.
Supports assessment for myoglobinuria even though the urine currently appears clear.
Reasonable to exclude hypothyroid myopathy as a contributor, but not the acute priority.
Not indicated in the acute setting; the picture is a drug-induced myopathy and biopsy is not first-line.
